meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about a lack of portfolio. Learn to build a high-converting SaaS starter kit using Antigravity and Claude Code to showcase your engineering skills.
content.answer_block: Overcome the 'no portfolio' trap by building a modular SaaS starter kit. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid architecture and Claude Code for iterative development, you create a tangible asset that demonstrates your ability to ship scalable, revenue-ready software—the ultimate proof for prospective clients and investors alike.
content.intro:
The cold reality of the SaaS industry is that nobody buys your potential; they buy your output. When your portfolio is empty, you are invisible to the market. You are stuck in a loop of 'need experience to get work, need work to get experience,' while competitors who ship daily are capturing the market share you deserve.

But the tide is turning. By building a high-performance SaaS starter kit, you stop being a dreamer and start being a founder. This isn't just about writing code; it's about architecting a machine that proves you can solve complex problems, manage user authentication, and deploy production-grade software. It is your ticket from anonymous developer to verified builder.

steps.title: Define the SaaS Architecture
steps.description: Establish the foundational stack. Focus on a robust auth flow and database schema that proves you understand enterprise-grade security and state management.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, scalable SaaS architecture diagram and base code structure using Next.js 14, Supabase, and Stripe integration. Focus on modularity for future feature expansion.

steps.title: Implement Role-Based Access Control
steps.description: Showcase your ability to handle complex user permissions. This is a critical requirement for any B2B SaaS product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust RBAC middleware that handles three tiers: Admin, Editor, and Viewer. Ensure the logic is extensible for future custom roles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement RBAC logic in middleware.ts with secure cookies'

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Billing Flows
steps.description: Monetization is the core of SaaS. Building a clean checkout and subscription management UI proves you can build revenue-generating systems.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe webhook handler and a subscription management dashboard component that handles upgrades, downgrades, and cancellations.

steps.title: Build a Documentation Engine
steps.description: A great developer documents their work. Adding an auto-generated API doc section adds massive professional value to your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a documentation engine that pulls from JSDoc comments to generate a searchable API reference page within the dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add automated documentation generation to the /docs route'

steps.title: Deploy and Portfolio-Ready Polish
steps.description: Finalize the production build and ensure the code quality is impeccable. This version will be the centerpiece of your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a final code audit, optimize bundle size for performance, and generate a README.md that explains the technical decisions made.

pro_tips: Always include a 'Data Export' feature in your starter kit; it signifies you understand user ownership and GDPR compliance.
Use a 'Feature Flag' pattern from day one to show you can iterate without breaking production environments.
Prioritize a 'Dark Mode' toggle; it is a simple feature that signals high-level UI/UX attention to detail.
Write your commit messages in conventional-commits format to prove you understand team collaboration standards.
Include a 'Performance Metrics' dashboard to show you care about the latency and end-user experience of your applications.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is live, document the 'Why' behind your architecture in a technical blog post and link it directly to your repository. Start reaching out to potential clients by showing them the live demo rather than a list of skills.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the technical edge to build a SaaS that converts visitors into users. Don't let your skills stay hidden behind a lack of portfolio. Start building your infrastructure, prove your worth, and launch your first revenue-generating product today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
